title: Use a query-group ranker and AUC-safe OOF stacking instead of raw probability averaging
summary: AUC is fundamentally about ordering, and LightGBM/CatBoost ranking objectives can learn which laps within a driver-race or driver-stint are most stop-like.
rationale: AUC is fundamentally about ordering, and LightGBM/CatBoost ranking objectives can learn which laps within a driver-race or driver-stint are most stop-like. For blending, scikit-learn notes isotonic can change AUC by introducing ties, while beta calibration is a stronger parametric alternative than plain sigmoid on skewed score distributions.
implementation_hint: Train a ranker with queries like `(Race-Year, Driver, Stint)` and relevance increasing as the next stop approaches; generate out-of-fold rank scores, calibrate each specialist with sigmoid or beta on grouped OOF predictions, and train a low-capacity meta-learner on OOF predictions only.
expected_effect: Should improve pit-window ordering while preserving ensemble discrimination better than ad hoc averaging or isotonic-heavy post-processing.
risk: Ranking labels need careful construction in multi-stop races, and isotonic should be avoided unless tie-induced AUC changes are acceptable.
